description: Figure 11 presents the variation of damping ratios as a function of manganese content for as-cast Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C alloy sheet castings, where x ranges from 5 to 40 mass%. The graph displays a single descending curve showing that the damping ratio decreases monotonically from approximately 0.060 at 5 mass% Mn to about 0.042 at 40 mass% Mn. According to the caption and citation context, these values were extracted from frequency response functions (FRF) using the half-power point method during experimental modal analysis. The data points correspond to specific alloy compositions: 0.0603 (5.12% Mn), 0.0589 (9.85% Mn), 0.0561 (14.89% Mn), 0.0539 (20.06% Mn), 0.0518 (24.87% Mn), 0.0491 (30.45% Mn), 0.0446 (34.88% Mn), and 0.0417 (39.73% Mn). This inverse relationship between damping capacity and Mn content correlates with the microstructural evolution from duplex austenite-ferrite phases to single austenite phase as Mn increases, indicating that the damping behavior is strongly influenced by phase constitution in this Fe-Al-Mn-C system.
